title: End-to-end hello-world example with getting-started guide
task: Create a complete, reproducible hello-world example that takes a new user from zero to a running Aion workflow. This is the first thing someone sees when evaluating Aion — it must be excellent, clear, and work out of the box. The example should demonstrate: writing a Gleam workflow with aion_flow, packaging it into a .aion archive, starting the server, deploying the package, starting a workflow instance, and observing the result.

requirements.description: Create a Gleam workflow project at examples/hello-world/ that uses the aion_flow SDK. The workflow should accept a JSON input with a 'name' field, dispatch one activity (a greeting generator), and return a greeting string. Include a gleam.toml with aion_flow as a path dependency.
requirements.acceptance: gleam build succeeds in the examples/hello-world/ directory and produces .beam files

requirements.description: Create a small Rust binary at examples/hello-world/packager/ (or a build.sh script using cargo-script or similar) that reads the compiled .beam files and the workflow manifest, then uses aion-package's PackageBuilder to produce a hello-world.aion file. The Manifest should declare the entry module/function, the input/output schemas, and the activity list.
requirements.acceptance: Running the packager produces a valid .aion file that can be loaded by the engine

requirements.description: Create examples/hello-world/README.md that walks through the complete path: prerequisites (gleam CLI, cargo, rust toolchain), building the .aion package, starting the dev server with the repo-root dev-config.json, deploying the .aion package, starting a workflow instance via curl, and observing the result on the dashboard and via the list/describe API. Every command should be copy-pasteable.
requirements.acceptance: A developer with gleam and cargo installed can follow the README from scratch and see a workflow run

requirements.description: Create a Python worker script at examples/hello-world/worker.py that uses the aion-worker-python SDK to register the 'greet' activity handler and connect to the running server. The handler takes {name: string} and returns {greeting: 'Hello, <name>! Welcome to Aion.'}
requirements.acceptance: python worker.py connects to the server and serves the greeting activity

context: The aion_flow Gleam SDK is at gleam/aion_flow/ — it provides workflow.define() for entry contracts, workflow.run() for activity dispatch, and deterministic time/signal/query helpers. The aion-package crate at crates/aion-package/ has PackageBuilder::new(manifest, beams) and write_to_path(). The Manifest struct needs entry_module, entry_function, and activity declarations. The server at crates/aion-server/ takes a JSON config — a working dev-config.json already exists at the repo root. The Python worker SDK is at sdks/python/aion-worker/ with session/loop/reconnect. The server listens on gRPC :50051 and HTTP :8080. Auth uses bearer token 'dev-token'. The workflow HTTP API is at /workflows/start, /workflows/list, /workflows/describe (all POST with JSON body).
